**AI CAPEX CYCLE – SIMILARITIES WITH INTERNET BOOM**  

**KEY DEVELOPMENT**  
- Current AI capex cycle resembles the 1990s internet boom.  
- Big spend on compute and AI infrastructure is underway.  

**SIMILAR PATTERNS**  
- Large capex in datacenters and cloud compute.  
- Falling costs and better model efficiency (cheaper tokens, stronger models).  
- Rapid adoption and new use cases across industries.  

**KEY DIFFERENCE THIS TIME**  
- Digital infrastructure is already in place.  
- Adoption can be much faster than in the early internet days.  

**CYCLE VIEW**  
- AI cycle is real and structurally strong.  
- Valuations may correct, but long‑term adoption likely stays intact.  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive for infrastructure and platform leaders.  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- AI capex is like the internet build‑out, but on a ready‑made base.  
- Winners will likely be infrastructure enablers and scalable platforms.

**BLACKROCK'S $12.5T vs INDIA'S $4.13T GDP**  

**ASSET GIANTS SCALE**  
- BlackRock manages $12.5 trillion AUM  
- Vanguard $10.1T, Fidelity $5.9T, State Street $4.67T  
- These 4 firms exceed most nations' annual output  

**INDIA'S REALITY**  
- Nominal GDP $4.13T equals Fidelity's AUM alone  
- State Street manages more than India's total production  
- 1.4B Indians' output matches one firm's client assets  

**GROWTH TRAJECTORY**  
- India hit 4th largest economy, passed Japan mid-2025  
- Targeting 3rd largest by 2030 at $6.3T GDP  
- GDP nearly tripled in 10 years (Rs 106L Cr → Rs 331L Cr)  

**GLOBAL LEADERSHIP**  
- #1 vaccine producer (60% global supply)  
- #3 PPP economy; digital economy $1T by 2029  
- 2nd largest workforce: 607M workers (+46.6M in 1 year)  

**THE BIG LESSON**  
- BlackRock took 30+ years from 1988 to $12.5T  
- Built on developed markets; India's emerging turn next  
- Patience and conviction close massive gaps

**BELRISE INDUSTRIES: JM FINANCIAL UPDATE**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- Maintain BUY; TP ₹225 (≈22% upside from ₹184)  
- Valuation: 25x FY28E EPS  
- Current valuation: ~20.2x P/E | ~2.5x P/BV  

**KEY DEVELOPMENT (AEROSPACE ENTRY)**  
- Acquiring 100% stake in UK-based Chester Hall Precision Engineering  
- Focus: aerospace, aviation, space & defence components  
- Expertise in titanium & high-grade aluminium precision parts  

**DEAL DETAILS**  
- Deal value: GBP 13.2 mn (~₹163 Cr)  
- Valuation: ~6.1x FY26E EV/EBITDA  
- Structure: Deferred + earn-out linked  
- Closure expected within ~20 business days  

**TARGET COMPANY SNAPSHOT**  
- CY24 revenue: GBP 19.9 mn  
- CY25E revenue: GBP 18.5 mn  
- EBITDA: GBP 2.1–2.2 mn (margin ~11–12%)  

**STRATEGIC RATIONALE**  
- Diversification beyond auto into high-entry-barrier aerospace  
- Access to global OEM relationships & certifications  
- Entry into long-cycle, high-margin precision manufacturing  

**FINANCIAL IMPACT**  
- EPS & RoCE accretive from Day 1  
- Near-term contribution modest (~2% of earnings/revenue)  
- Platform for long-term aerospace scaling  

**CORE BUSINESS OUTLOOK**  
- Revenue: ₹7,484 Cr → ₹12,288 Cr (FY24–28E CAGR: low-mid teens)  
- EBITDA: ₹928 Cr → ₹1,561 Cr (margin ~12–13%)  
- PAT: ₹326 Cr → ₹807 Cr  
- ROIC improving: ~11.6% → ~15%  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
- Net debt/equity: 1.0x (FY25) → net cash by FY28  
- EV/EBITDA: 18.8x → 10.1x (deleveraging benefit)  

**ESTIMATE CHANGES**  
- Revenue ↑ 1.8–4.2% (FY26–28E)  
- EBITDA/PAT marginal tweaks (-0.6% to +4.8%)  
- EPS: ₹7.6 (FY27E) → ₹9.1 (FY28E)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strategic shift from pure auto-anc to aerospace diversification  
- Long-term value creation via high-margin, high-entry-barrier segment

**LG ELECTRONICS INDIA (LGEL): CENTRUM UPDATE**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- Maintain BUY; TP ₹1,850 (≈18% upside from ₹1,564)  
- Valuation: 40x FY28E EPS ₹46.2 (PEG ~1.3x)  
- Strong earnings visibility with no estimate changes  

**FY26 OUTLOOK & CURRENT TRENDS**  
- FY26 sales: Flat to marginal growth YoY  
- EBITDA margin: Double-digit maintained  
- Price hikes: AC +7–9%, WM +2%, Refrigerator +2%  
- TV: No price hike (new tech launches from Apr’26)  
- Strong demand: TV & WM double-digit growth; AC already above last year base  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Portfolio expansion:  
  - Chest freezers (₹30 bn market)  
  - Fixed-speed AC relaunch (ODM route)  
  - Premium India-made products  
- B2B segment growing ~20%; now ~9–10% of sales  
- VRF leadership: ~25% market share  

**PREMIUMIZATION STRATEGY**  
- Premium mix ~28–29% vs industry ~14–15%  
- “LG Essential” range to drive volumes without margin dilution  

**EXPORTS & LOCALISATION**  
- Exports: 6–7% → target to double by FY27  
- Expansion into US & Europe markets  
- Localisation: ~55–58% (ex-TV ~65%)  
- Improves margin stability & supply chain control  

**CAPEX & EXPANSION**  
- New Sri City plant:  
  - AC: FY27 | WM: FY28 | Refrigerator: FY29  
  - Backward integration (AC compressors)  
- Enhances logistics efficiency (South = ~40% sales)  

**RISK MANAGEMENT**  
- LPG shortage manageable (shift to PNG/acetylene)  
- Vendor disruptions supported by company  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK (FY24–28E)**  
- Revenue: ₹21,352 Cr → ₹32,954 Cr  
- EBITDA margin: 10.2% (FY26) → 13.3% (FY28)  
- RoE: Strong at ~28–33%  
- Net cash balance sheet + strong FCF  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong premiumization + B2B growth + exports drive long-term story  
- Temporary margin dip in FY26, followed by strong recovery

**TRIVENI TURBINE: GEOJIT UPDATE**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- Rating: Accumulate; TP ₹535 (≈15% upside from ₹465)  
- Valuation at 33x FY28E EPS (discount to 5-yr avg P/E)  
- Discount reflects near-term export/order finalisation risks  

**Q3FY26 PERFORMANCE**  
- Revenue: ₹624 Cr (↑ 24% YoY | ↑ 23% QoQ)  
- Growth driven by exports (+54% YoY); domestic ↓ ~6% YoY  
- Product sales ↑ ~49% YoY; Aftermarket ↓ 22% YoY (order deferment)  
- EBITDA margin: 21.5% (↓ 22 bps YoY)  
- Gross margin: 46.8% (↓ 269 bps YoY)  

**ORDER BOOK & OUTLOOK**  
- Order book: ₹1,986 Cr (↑ 9% YoY)  
- Domestic inflow ↑ 32% YoY; export inflow ↓ 34% YoY  
- Q4FY26 expected to be record quarter (revenue + profitability)  
- FY26 outlook: Double-digit topline growth  

**GLOBAL EXPANSION**  
- US subsidiary to move beyond breakeven in FY27  
- Meaningful contributor from FY28 onwards  
- South Africa unit expanding in refurbishment market  

**STRUCTURAL DRIVERS**  
- Renewable energy & energy efficiency demand  
- Waste-to-energy & decentralised power solutions  
- US tariff cuts supportive for future orders  
- Middle East tensions remain key overhang  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK (FY26–28E)**  
- Revenue: ₹2,122 Cr → ₹2,969 Cr (CAGR ~18%)  
- EBITDA: ₹456 Cr → ₹635 Cr (margin ~21–21.5%)  
- PAT: ₹374 Cr → ₹509 Cr  
- ROE: ~27% → ~23%  
- Balance sheet: Zero net debt  

**RISKS / ADJUSTMENTS**  
- FY26/FY27 estimates cut due to slower export order finalisation  
- Geopolitical risks impacting exports  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive (with near-term caution)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong structural growth story with high ROE & zero debt  
- Near-term export uncertainty priced in; domestic pipeline supportive

**AXIS BANK: VENTURA BUY INITIATION UPDATE**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- Upgraded to BUY; TP ₹1,934 (≈54% upside from ₹1,253)  
- Valuation: 2.3x FY28E P/BV  
- FY28E estimates: EPS ₹94.7 | BVPS ₹834.1  
- Implies ~12.5x P/E and ~1.4x P/B at TP  

**GROWTH & PROFITABILITY**  
- Advances growth: ~13–14% CAGR (FY25–28E)  
- Deposits growth: ~15% YoY  
- NII CAGR: ~10.2% → ₹72,788 Cr by FY28E  
- PAT: ~₹31,364 Cr by FY28E  
- RoAA ~1.4% | RoAE ~12%  

**MARGINS PROFILE**  
- NIM guided at ~3.5–3.8%  
- PPOP margin ~29%  
- Stable margin outlook through cycle  

**FRANCHISE STRENGTH**  
- SBB/SME/Mid-Corp growing ~22% YoY  
- Now ~24% of total loan mix (higher yield, granular)  
- Balanced business model: lending + fee income  

**DIGITAL LEADERSHIP**  
- ~39% UPI market share  
- Axis Mobile ~15 mn MAUs  
- Strong fee income mix (~92% granular fees)  

**ASSET QUALITY & BALANCE SHEET**  
- GNPA ~1.40% | NNPA ~0.43% (FY28E)  
- PCR ~72% (strong provisioning buffer)  
- CET-1 at 14.5% → strong capital position  
- Corporate book largely de-risked  

**RE-RATING TRIGGERS**  
- Transition from recovery to structural growth story  
- Strong digital ecosystem + granular loan mix  
- Stable asset quality + improving return ratios  
- Potential value unlocking from subsidiaries  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Axis evolving into high-quality growth franchise  
- Combination of growth + asset quality + digital strength supports valuation re-rating

**VALUATION & TARGET**

- 25x FY28E EPS Rs 20.2; FY25-28E CAGR: 14% revenue/17% PAT  

**BUSINESS MOAT**  
- #1 2W braking systems (~50% share brake shoes/ABS)

**SEGMENT GROWTH**

**MARGINS & ALLOCATION**

**BIKAJI FOODS: SYSTEMATIX BUY INITIATION**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- BUY initiated; TP Rs 750 (22% upside from Rs 616)  
- 52x FY28E EPS; FY25-28E CAGR: 15% revenue, 22% EPS  

**FRANCHISE STRENGTH**  
- #3 organized ethnic snacks (8% share); #1 Bikaneri bhujia  
- 9MFY26 mix: 64% ethnic snacks, 13% sweets, 8% western  
- FY20-25: 19% sales CAGR; RoE/RoCE ~18% FY26E  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Distribution: 335k direct outlets → 500k in 3 years  
- Core markets 68% sales; others >30% CAGR  
- Adjacencies (frozen, QSR, bakery): 8% → meaningful scale  
- Exports: 6% → double in 3-4 years (43 countries)  

**MARGIN & FCF**  
- EBITDA margin +345 bps to 13.9% FY25-28E  
- 60% revenue from premium family packs  
- A&P 2-2.5% sales; FCF ~Rs 9.7 bn FY26-28

**KSB: MNCL BUY RATING RAISED**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- BUY retained; TP Rs 940 (from Rs 920); 24% upside from Rs 760  
- 45x Dec'27E EPS; CY25-28 CAGR: 13% revenue/EBITDA/PAT  

**Q4CY25 RESULTS**  
- Revenue Rs 7,840 mn (+7.9% YoY); EBITDA Rs 1,296 mn (16.5% margin)  
- PAT Rs 810 mn (+10.8% YoY); hit by Rs 255 mn labour code charge  
- CY25: Revenue Rs 26,957 mn (+6.4%); EBITDA margin 13.9%  

**ORDER BOOK & GROWTH**  
- Rs 25.8 bn book; Rs 12.8 bn NPCIL nuclear pumps  
- NPCIL testing starts March 22; Rs 2-3 bn annual potential  
- Ex-nuclear intake 19% CAGR; solar Rs 2,450 mn (+20% FY26 guide)  

**MARGIN DRIVERS**  
- Guides 13-14% EBITDA medium-term  
- Mix boost: nuclear, aftermarket (15% revenue, high margin)  
- Vs KBL: Superior Q4 margins (16.5% vs 12.7%)  

**RISKS**  
- Middle East gas issues for foundry; export exposure  
- Net cash; ROE 17-19%; capex 3.5% sales

@LongTermAlpha

**Power Sector Update – Demand & Pricing Trends**

**Demand Trends**
• February 2026 demand grew modestly at +1.5% YoY
• Early March (1–17) shows revival with +3.4% YoY growth

**Tariff Movement**
• DAM tariffs averaged ₹3.58/kWh in Feb’26 (↓ 18% YoY)
• March MTD tariffs rebounded to ₹4.55/kWh (↑ 4% YoY)
• Improvement driven by pickup in demand

**Capacity & Renewable Mix**
• Installed capacity reached 524 GW as of Feb’26
• Record addition of 54 GW over last 12 months
• Renewables contributed 100% of recent capacity addition
• RE share increased to 41% (vs 36% YoY)

**Overall View**
• Short-term demand softness seen in Feb, but recovery visible in March
• Rising renewable share continues to reshape power mix
• Positive demand outlook with improving pricing trend

**Overall View**
• Strong growth visibility with margin support
• Positive outlook backed by expansion strategy
